problem text: You are on a random journey along a discrete line with $11$ points, from $0$ to $10$. Each step, you have an equal chance of moving up or down. Once you hit either $10$ or $0$, your movement stops. If you start at position $6$, what is the likelihood of arriving at $10$ before hitting $0$?
problem solution: This represents a typical symmetric random walk scenario. The likelihood of arriving at $10$ before reaching $0$ when commencing at $6$ is $\dfrac{6}{10}$. This is found by comparing the distance from the lower boundary $(0)$ to the starting position $(6)$ against the total span of the interval $(10)$.
